english: bo (bo) Sch.; rta-mog-ro (rta mog ro) Glr. a yellowish-brown horse; rta zag-pa (rta zag pa) Sch. a horse having gray and white spots; rta ól-ba (rta 'ol ba) Mil., Ld.-Glr., a black horse; rta ra-rá (rta ra ra) Sch. a yellow-dun horse; rta rag-pa (rta rag pa) Ld. a tawny horse (Sch.: 'a white and red spotted horse'); rta rag-rág (rta rag rag) an ash-gray horse; rta rag-sér (rta rag ser), or rta ser-sér (rta ser ser) Sch. a yellowish-red horse; rta sram-srám (rta sram sram) Sch. a gray horse with a black mane and tail.

1. perpetual, constant, lasting, eternal. 2. perpetuity, duration to all futurity, a quality which, acc. to Buddhist views, can be ascribed only to the vacuum, to absolute emptiness, the stoṅ-pa-nyid (stong pa nyid); mi rtag-pa (mi rtag pa) not durable, perishable; de yaṅ mi-rtag tsul-du hda (de yang mi rtag tshul du 'da') this, too, is subject to the law of perishableness Mil.; mi rtag-pai ćos (mi rtag pa'i chos) the principle of transitoriness; rtag-par 'dzin-pa (rtag par 'dzin pa) to look upon (transitory things, i.e. the world) as lasting, and hence: to be worldly-minded Glr.; as partic. one that is earthly-minded, a worldling; nyal-ba-la rtag-pa (nyal ba la rtag pa) steady in lying, i.e. disposed to lie down, to be continually at rest, Sty.; rtag-ćad (rtag chad) lasting and transitory, frq.; rtag-par (rtag par), or more frq. rtag-tu (rtag tu), always, i.e. 1. continually, 2. at each time (Dzl. 2, 5); rtag-tu-ba (rtag tu ba) perpetuity, eternity Cs. - rtag-po (rtag po), Ld. *stags-po* (*stags po*), lasting, durable, reliable, rtag-brtan (rtag brtan) id. C.; rtag-snyóm-la (rtag snyoms la) C. adv. uniformly, equally. - rtag-rés k'ór-ba (rtag res 'khor ba) Sch.: a constant change (?).

(cf. rtógs-pa (rtogs pa)) 1. resp. pyag-rtags (phyag rtags), sign, token, mark, characteristic, *tag-zi* (*tag zi*) W., *tags-pa* (*tags pa*) Ld., id.; rtags byéd-pa (rtags byed pa), vulg. *tag rgyáb-pa* (*tag rgyab pa*) to make a mark; rab-tu byuṅ-bai rtags yód-pa (rab tu byung ba'i rtags yod pa) (partic.) one having the outward marks of an ecclesiastic Glr.; bkra-śis rtags (bkra shis rtags) v. bkra-śis (bkra shis); omen, prognostic, = ltas (ltas), bu-mo skyé-bai rtags (bu mo skye ba'i rtags) a prognostic of a girl being born Med.; proof of a thing, c. genit., frq.; mṅon-rtágs (mngon rtags) Dzl. id.; proof, argument, evidence, *ći tags-pa-ne zum* (*ci tags pa ne zum*) Ld. upon what evidence have they seized him? *tags-pa žig yos* (*tags pa zhig yos*) you must prove it, *tags-pa-aṅ mi dug* (*tags pa ang mi dug*) there is no trace, no evidence, left. - 2. inference, deduction Was. (320). - 3. the black, the centre of a target, W. *tág-la ćug-će* (*tag la cug ce*) to take for a mark. - 4. sexual organ, organ of generation, rtags-sam bhá-ga (rtags sam bha ga) as two synonyms for the same thing Wdn., pó-rtags (pho rtags), mó-rtags (mo rtags) frq. - 5. gift, present, resp. pyag-rtágs (phyag rtags). - 6. any mark for denoting grammatical distinctions, such as terminations etc., ni (ni) f.; rtags 'jug-pa (rtags 'jug pa) using such marks, making grammatical distinctions, seems to imply about the same thing as our etymology, the etymological part of grammar. - rtags-yig (rtags yig) 1. stamp, type (?) Cs. 2. letter of recommendation, credentials W. - *tag-ril* (*tag ril*) W., lot, *tag-ril taṅ-će* (*tag ril tang ce*) to cast or draw lots (a half-religious proceeding) cf. rgyan (rgyan).

also rtab-rtáb-pa (rtab rtab pa), and stáb-pa (stab pa), to be in a hurry, to be confused, frightened, in a state of alarm, e.g. of fowl frightened by some cause (Zam. = bréd-pa (bred pa)); rtab-po (rtab po) adj.; stab-stáb-por soṅ-nas (stab stab por song nas) having become quite startled and confounded Pth.; rtab-rtób (rtab rtob) sbst., rtab-rtób-tu naṅ-du pyin-te (rtab rtob tu nang du phyin te) she ran into the house in haste (full of joy) Mil.; rtab-rtáb-la ra mi 'dren (rtab rtab la ra mi 'dren) I cannot help you with such speed Mil. nt. It is also spelled brtabs-pa (brtabs pa).

(in more recent literature and col.) what is behind or after, with regard to space, and more particularly to time, rtiṅ-du (rting du), rtiṅ-la (rting la), rtiṅ-na (rting na) adv. afterwards, rtiṅ-du bćós-so (rting du bcos so) they were made afterwards, were added later Glr.; postp. c. genit., or less corr. c. accus., after; byon rtiṅ-la (byon rting la) after their appearance Pth., byuṅ-rtiṅ (byung rting) after he has come Mil.; de-rtiṅ-la (de rting la) after that Glr.; *tiṅ-ne daṅ-će* (*ting ne dang ce*) W. to follow, to come after or later; rtiṅ-ma (rting ma) adj. and sbst. the last Tar.; gtam-gyi rtiṅ-ma yin (gtam gyi rting ma yin) this is my last, my farewell-speech Glr.: without ma: *dus tiṅ žig-na* (*dus ting zhig na*) W. some day hereafter, some future day; *tiṅ-ma žag, tiṅ-ma nyi-* (*ting ma zhag, ting ma nyi-*)
